mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already below the recent policy peak, equity discount rates ease and the present value of near-term cash flows rises, so S&P 500 returns over the next month should be positive.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative when FEDFUNDS is flat or declining, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A move in FEDFUNDS above 4.5% indicates restrictive policy is tightening financial conditions, which compresses valuation multiples and should pressure S&P 500 returns lower over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:policy rate at 4.1% as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the post-trigger average return is not below unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE after a low-unemployment regime signals labor market deterioration and slower earnings momentum, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average triggered return is not materially below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS is declining while UNRATE is rising, payroll losses and higher slack jointly confirm an earnings-downgrade regime, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up signal does not outperform either series alone, or if 5-day forward returns are not negative in a majority of triggered joint episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating RSAFS reflects stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue growth, which should support equity earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if higher RSAFS growth is not associated with higher subsequent 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the relation flips in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST below trend signals weakening housing demand and softer cyclically sensitive activity, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-deterioration triggers are followed by non-negative 60-day SPX returns in most cases, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional 60-day mean.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL stays contained, firms are expanding real output without renewed inflation pressure, which should support risk sentiment and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint industrial-production-up and inflation-contained episodes do not outperform single-series signals, or if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery, improving GDPC1 combined with rising PAYEMS signals synchronized real activity and income growth, which should drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDP-and-payroll expansion signal fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses explanatory power after controlling for either proxy alone.
